name: PHP
scope: A person with BASIC proficiency level in PHP as a competency should be able to read-write procedural and simple OOP code using PHP 8.x features (scalar/union types, constructor promotion, named args, null-safe operator, attributes), strict_types, namespaces, visibility, autoloading (PSR-4), SPL, superglobals, arrays/strings/DateTime/filesystem helpers, and understand request life-cycle in CLI or FPM. They debug compile-time/run-time issues with exceptions, logging, Xdebug or IDE debuggers, grasp copy-vs-reference semantics, static variables, and the difference between errors and exceptions. They design small domain models with interfaces, traits, abstract classes, magic methods, value objects, DTOs, and basic factories while following SRP and composition-over-inheritance. They install/manage dependencies with Composer, use Git branching/merges, follow PSR-1/PSR-12 enforced by phpcs or php-cs-fixer, and run phpstan/psalm (level 3-5). They write PHPUnit or Pest unit tests, view coverage, integrate linting/testing/analysis in CI pipelines, and package code for shared hosting, containers (PHP-FPM), or serverless. They handle HTTP parsing, routing, controllers, templating, and response emission in native PHP or micro/major frameworks (Laravel, Symfony, Slim, Laminas) and integrate libraries for HTTP clients, auth, mailing, or background jobs. They perform CRUD with PDO, lightweight query builders or active-record ORM, use parameterized queries, transactions, and serialize/deserialize JSON/XML/CSV. They apply security basics—validation, escaping, prepared statements, hashing+salting, CSRF tokens, secret management via env vars—and recognize when to escalate deeper concerns. They profile code, spot N+1 or loop bottlenecks, leverage OPcache, and stream large data to manage memory. They document via DocBlocks, README, changelogs, ADRs, estimate tasks, and participate in peer reviews with clear commit messages. They keep abreast of PHP deprecations and deliver routine business features under senior guidance.
long_scope:
Competency Scope  
PHP – BASIC Proficiency (≈ 1–2 years hands-on experience)

Purpose  
This scope defines the knowledge, skills, and expected outcomes for a practitioner who is no longer a novice but is still early in the professional journey. The individual can deliver routine business features with guidance, understands the language core, and applies foundational engineering practices. Depth is limited to standard scenarios, but the person can already produce production-grade PHP code when guard-railed by reviews, guidelines, and documented processes.

1. Core Language Fluency  
   • Reads and writes procedural and simple object-oriented PHP confidently.  
   • Applies current stable language version features up to the 8.x release line (scalar type declarations, union types, constructor property promotion, named arguments, null-safe operator, attributes) without needing deep metaprogramming knowledge.  
   • Consistently uses namespaces, visibility modifiers, strict_types, autoloading (PSR-4).  
   • Leverages built-in data types, operators, control structures, error suppression rules, and execution modes (CLI vs FPM).  
   • Understands the lifecycle of a PHP request, from script invocation to response emission.

2. Fundamental Run-time Concepts  
   • Differentiates between compile-time and run-time errors and resolves them using the standard error, exception, and logging subsystems.  
   • Explains reference vs copy semantics for primitives and arrays, scope rules, static variables, and superglobals.  
   • Works comfortably with the SPL, array and string helpers, DateTime API, and filesystem functions for everyday tasks.

3. Object-Oriented Building Blocks  
   • Designs small domain models and service classes following encapsulation, single responsibility, and composition over inheritance.  
   • Employs interfaces, traits, abstract classes, and magic methods with awareness of their trade-offs.  
   • Creates and consumes value objects, DTOs, and simple factories without advanced dependency injection patterns.

4. Standard Toolchain Usage  
   • Installs, configures, and updates project dependencies with Composer, adhering to semantic version constraints and autoload directives.  
   • Uses Git for daily branching, commits, rebasing, and merge-request workflows.  
   • Debugs through Xdebug or an IDE integrated debugger, sets breakpoints, inspects call stacks, and tracks variable changes.

5. HTTP, Web & I/O Handling  
   • Parses request data (query, form, JSON, file uploads), sanitizes, and validates it.  
   • Produces HTTP responses with correct status codes, headers, and content types.  
   • Implements routing, controller logic, and templating within a micro framework or native PHP, maintaining clear separation of concerns.

6. Persistence Layer Interaction  
   • Executes CRUD operations against relational databases through PDO and lightweight query builders or an ORM in active-record style.  
   • Writes parameterized statements, handles transactions for atomic changes, and manages connection errors.  
   • Serializes and deserializes state to formats like JSON, XML, or CSV when persisting or integrating with external services.

7. Security Foundation  
   • Applies input validation, output escaping, prepared statements, and hashing/salting of secrets to prevent injection, XSS, CSRF, insecure direct object references, and basic session fixation.  
   • Manages environment variables and configuration secrets without hard-coding credentials.

8. Code Quality & Testing  
   • Follows PSR-1/PSR-12 coding standards enforced by tools (PHP-CS-Fixer, phpcs).  
   • Writes and runs unit tests with PHPUnit or Pest covering happy paths and common edge cases.  
   • Generates coverage reports and interprets them to improve test suites.

9. Performance & Resource Awareness  
   • Profiles execution with built-in functions or external profilers to spot obvious bottlenecks (N+1 queries, unnecessary loops).  
   • Configures opcode caching (OPcache) and understands impact on deployment cycles.  
   • Handles memory-intensive tasks by chunking or streaming rather than loading entire payloads into memory.

10. Framework & Ecosystem Familiarity  
    • Crafts small applications or modules using at least one mainstream framework (Laravel, Symfony, Laminas, or Slim) limited to routing, controllers, models, views, and artisan/console tooling.  
    • Integrates third-party libraries for common concerns—HTTP clients, authentication, mailing, background jobs—while respecting framework conventions.

11. Continuous Integration & Delivery Basics  
    • Creates pipeline steps to run lint, static analysis (phpstan or psalm level 3-5), unit tests, and packaging tasks.  
    • Prepares application artefacts for deployment on shared hosting, containerized PHP-FPM, or serverless runtimes.  
    • Applies environment-specific configuration through .env files or CI/CD variables.

12. Documentation & Collaboration  
    • Produces in-code DocBlocks for classes, methods, and complex logic; maintains README, change logs, and basic ADRs.  
    • Participates in peer reviews, explains code decisions, and incorporates feedback.  
    • Breaks down requirements into technical tasks, estimates effort with guidance, and updates task boards.

13. Professional Mind-set  
    • Recognizes when to escalate architectural or security concerns to senior engineers.  
    • Practices iterative delivery, commits small cohesive changes, and writes meaningful commit messages.  
    • Keeps current with PHP release notes and deprecations to plan minor version upgrades responsibly.

A professional who meets this BASIC scope is dependable for well-defined product increments, follows established patterns, and lays solid groundwork for more advanced architectural and cross-domain responsibilities encountered at INTERMEDIATE level and beyond.
